title: Screen Time Without Guilt: A Family Plan That Kids Accept
excerpt: Set screen boundaries that are clear, fair, and far easier to enforce every day.
coverAlt: Child using tablet with parent nearby

content.text: Most parents are not trying to eliminate screens. They are trying to stop screens from controlling the daily rhythm of the house.
Inconsistency creates conflict. Kids can handle limits better when those limits are explicit, visible, and repeated.
Anchor screens to routine rather than behavior rewards to avoid emotional overvaluation.

items: Define when, where, and how long.
Write one family media rule card.

content.text: You do not need zero screens. You need clear boundaries and stronger connection habits around them.
